theme_description: Terminal/Hacker aesthetic combined with modern dev-tool minimalism (Linear/Vercel style). Deep space black with high-contrast borders and cyan phosphor accents.

headings.usage: Use for all main headers (H1, H2, H3). NEVER use Inter. Keep H1 fluid between text-4xl and text-6xl.

body.usage: Use for body copy, paragraphs, and standard UI text.

code.usage: Use for all code blocks, terminal mockups, inline code, and CLI install commands. Mandatory for the 'hacker/terminal' aesthetic.

colors.palette_strategy: Base is deep black (#0505). Cards are slightly elevated (#0D0D0D). Borders are stark and visible (#1F1F1F). The primary accent is 'Phosphor Cyan' (HSL: 189 100% 50% / #00E5FF) to simulate a retro-futuristic terminal glow without the cliché Matrix green. Use a muted lime/emerald for positive signals (like minimalism check passes). DO NOT use purple/violet.

visual_enhancers.surfaces: Grid Borders (The 'Technical' Look): Expose the skeleton. Container uses border-collapse style layout. Items have subtle borders on right and bottom. Dark gray surfaces with light subtle borders.
visual_enhancers.effects: Subtle scanlines on terminal sections. Glassmorphism for floating headers (bg-black/60 with backdrop-blur-xl and border-b border-white/10). No full-screen gradients.
visual_enhancers.icons: Use lucide-react for standard icons. Consider @phosphor-icons/react if more technical fidelity is needed. No emojis for icons.

grids_and_layouts.spacing: Generous Spacing Philosophy. Use 2-3x more spacing. Tailwind p-8, p-12, p-16 for containers. Cards must have internal padding (p-6 or p-8).
grids_and_layouts.bento_grid: Mode B: High Density Grid for the Code Playground and Analysis Tabs. Strict grid hierarchy, dense and compact spacing.
grids_and_layouts.alignment: Mobile-first responsive. Optical alignment over geometrical. Left-aligned/asymmetric layouts. Make the code panels feel like a real editor.

components.buttons: Sharp geometric edges or very subtle roundness (rounded-md max). Primary buttons have Cyan background with black text. Hover state: bg-cyan-400. Focus: ring-cyan-500. Add micro-interaction: active:scale-95.
components.tabs: Override Shadcn tabs to look like a modern code editor tab list (like VS Code). No pills, just flat tabs with a bottom active border (border-b-2 border-cyan-500) and sharp text color transition.
components.code_playground: Left pane: Code input editor with line numbers (simulated or real). Right pane: 4 Analysis Tabs. Must feel like a seamless IDE. Use JetBrains Mono.
components.terminal_cli: A block simulating a terminal window for the install commands. Include macOS style window controls (red/yellow/green dots) or minimalist square controls. Monospace font, glowing cyan prompt indicator ($).
components.model_selector: Shadcn Select or Dropdown. Style it like a status bar item at the bottom right of the 'editor' or top right. Models: Claude Sonnet 4.5, Gemini 3 Flash, GPT-5.2.

motion.transitions: Use specific properties (e.g., transition-colors, transition-transform duration-200 ease-in-out). NEVER use transition-all.
motion.animations: Staggered load animations for analysis results. Hover states on all interactive elements. Micro-interactions for buttons.
motion.reduced_motion: Respect prefers-reduced-motion by keeping animations minimal or togglable via CSS media queries.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text against dark backgrounds meets APCA standards. Light gray (text-gray-300) for body, pure white (text-white) for headings.
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid attributes (kebab-case, role-based).
accessibility.focus: Clear focus rings (ring-2 ring-cyan-500 ring-offset-2 ring-offset-black).

media.images: No stock photos required for this CLI/Dev Tool aesthetic. Rely on typography, grid lines, syntax highlighting, and layout for visual interest. Can use SVG abstract patterns or noise textures for backgrounds.

pages_and_features.landing_hero: H1: 'Less code. More impact.' or similar minimalist ethos. Subheading explaining CodeSage. Two primary CTAs: 'Install CLI' and 'VS Code Extension'.
pages_and_features.code_playground: Interactive section. Paste code -> get review. Tabs: (1) Code Review (bugs/security), (2) Line-by-line, (3) Library Suggestions (stars, install), (4) Minimalism Check.
pages_and_features.feature_cards: Bento grid explaining the open-source nature, github integration, and the 'powerful libraries over raw code' philosophy.
pages_and_features.footer: Simple, technical. Links to Docs, GitHub, npm, VS Code Marketplace.

instructions_to_main_agent: Force dark mode by overriding index.css variables to match the Electric Neon + Retro-Futurism aesthetic.
Implement the UI using raw HTML/Tailwind for the landing page structure, and Shadcn components for the interactive playground (Tabs, Select, Buttons, Cards).
DO NOT use generic centered layouts or simplistic purple/violet gradients.
Apply 'data-testid' to all buttons, tabs, and form inputs.
Ensure the code playground looks like a legitimate code editor (e.g., VS Code or Linear's internal tools).
The analysis output should display diffs or minimal code suggestions clearly, using JetBrains Mono.
Avoid the 'AI slop' look. No emojis for UI elements. Use sleek lucide-react icons (e.g., Terminal, Zap, Shield).
Create a noise/grain texture overlay using a base64 SVG or CSS to enhance the terminal/hacker vibe.
Do NOT use HTML <select> or <input type='date'>. Rely entirely on Shadcn components for complex interactions.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition (e.g., transition: all). Always add transitions for specific interactive elements.
You MUST NOT center align the app container (.App { text-align: center; }).
Always use modern button styles (sharp or rounded-md) with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states: separates good from extraordinary.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui. Create new matching components when needed.
